title: trichopus zeylanicus subsp. travancoricus
intro: Trichopus zeylanicus subsp. travancoricus, also known as "Indian ginseng" or "Dhullick," is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the family Trichopodaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress and promote overall well-being. The medicinal properties of Trichopus zeylanicus subsp. travancoricus include its ability to enhance physical and mental performance, reduce fatigue, and improve immune function. The plant is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is believed to have various health benefits, including improved memory, reduced inflammation, and enhanced antioxidant activity.
